refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency metric and its impact on safety-critical teleoperation, focusing on measurable performance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data and validate p95 latency ≤ 150 ms during teleoperation scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting to detect violations of latency thresholds.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes close to the vehicle to reduce processing delays.
DP3: Design a dedicated teleoperation communication stack with minimal buffering and efficient serialization.
DP4: Integrate latency monitoring and adaptive control to maintain latency within limits.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.

potential_sources: Coupling between network protocol optimization and teleoperation command processing.
Dependency on network conditions and vehicle communication hardware.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates network latency reduction, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk that network congestion or hardware failures may increase latency beyond threshold.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding DP2 (edge computing) if network optimization alone is insufficient.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for implementing teleoperation communication protocols, latency optimization, and command/telemetry handling.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation commands and telemetry data over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides latency and performance data to monitoring modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands from operator control modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope but less focused on teleoperation specifics; higher diffusion risk.
Vehicle hardware handles command execution but not communication protocol optimization.
Highest containment score; centralizes teleoperation latency management and communication.
Focuses on safety alerts, not communication latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes latency optimization, minimizing diffusion and improving ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules.
Risk of latency monitoring data not being timely shared with Safety Services.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and monitoring data flows between Teleoperation and other modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP1 directly addresses the refined FR sub-requirement of minimizing communication latency with clear measurable criteria.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if latency monitoring is not fully integrated with Safety Services.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for DP1 with minimal diffusion; interfaces are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry interface and network link monitoring under test conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of latency optimization and monitoring within Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Enhance interface definitions for latency monitoring data sharing with Safety Services.
Consider fallback mechanisms if network conditions degrade beyond agreed parameters.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the requirement with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. Verification and ownership are well defined with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting to detect violations of latency thresholds.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement required protocol optimizations.
open_risks: Network congestion or hardware failures may increase latency.
Interface complexity with Safety Services for latency alerts.
evidence_needed: Latency performance test results under representative network conditions.
Interface validation between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
